title: Magnificently Garlicky American Pork Skillet
description: A garlicky and savory American dish featuring pork, perfect for a special occasion, this pescatarian-friendly recipe is sure to impress with its glassy-jelly texture from the reduced sauce.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the pork to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the skillet and set aside.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the white wine to the skillet and bring to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Add the chicken broth, honey, thyme, salt, and pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Return the pork to the skillet and simmer, uncovered, until the sauce has thickened and the pork is cooked through, about 10 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool slightly, allowing the sauce to reduce and develop a glassy-jelly texture.
Serve the pork and sauce hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
